app.title: Cyprus Citizenship Eligibility
app.subtitle: Estimate your naturalization eligibility and prepare supporting records
app.disclaimer: This tool provides an informal estimate only. It is not legal advice. Always verify results with a licensed Cyprus immigration lawyer before relying on any date or figure shown here.

route.title: Choose your route and language level
route.description: Your route determines the cumulative-years requirement and which form you'll file.
route.fastTrackDesc: Form M127. 3 years (B1 Greek) or 4 years (A2 Greek) within a rolling 10-year window, plus a final 1-year anniversary period.
route.standardDesc: Form M127. 7 years within a rolling 10-year window, plus a final 1-year anniversary period. B1 Greek required.
route.marriageDesc: Form M125. 3 years married and 2 years of cumulative residence during the marriage. Final-period rules are unconfirmed — verify with counsel.
route.greekLevelDescription: Required for fast-track and standard routes.
route.greekA2: A2 (fast-track only — raises cumulative requirement to 4 years)
route.greekExempt: Exempt (May 2024: Greek-taught school-leaving certificate or degree)
route.marriageNotice: Final-period rules for the marriage route are not confirmed by research. This app calculates only the flat 3-years-married + 2-years-residence rule. Verify all other aspects with counsel.

personal.title: Personal details
personal.description: These details drive your calculation and appear on the exported record.
personal.applicationDateDescription: The date you plan to submit your application. All calculations run backward from this date. This date itself is never counted as a day of presence.

arc.title: ARC and receipt dates
arc.description: The ARC issuing date controls when your countable presence begins.
arc.arcDateDescription: By default, physical presence is counted only from the day AFTER this date (a conservative measure). Use the toggle below to include the ARC day itself.
arc.includeArcDate: Include the ARC issuing date itself as a day of presence
arc.firstReceiptWasBcs: First receipt was a BCS receipt
arc.referenceOnlyNote: These receipt dates are stored for your reference table only. They do not affect the eligibility calculation.

travel.title: Travel log
travel.description: Log every arrival and departure to and from Cyprus. The departure day itself counts as absent; the arrival day counts as present.
travel.presetDescription: These optional dates are shown for your own reference in the exported record. They are never used in the eligibility calculation.
travel.orderWarning: Events must alternate departure / arrival in date order for the calculation to be accurate.
travel.importedFromEngine: Used directly in the eligibility calculation.

trips.title: Trips after the cumulative threshold
trips.description: Once your cumulative years requirement is met, every trip taken afterward is shown here automatically — it mirrors the “Trips after threshold” table in the exported record.
trips.noThresholdYet: The cumulative threshold has not been reached yet with your current data, so there is nothing to show here.

toggles.title: Toggles
toggles.arcToggleLabel: Include ARC issuing date as a day of presence
toggles.bcsDescription: Only years marked as “held BCS status” can receive the concession (fast-track route only). When ON, up to 90 absence days in that calendar year are forgiven for the cumulative tally.
toggles.concessionOn: Apply BCS concession (forgive up to 90 absence days)
toggles.description: Fine-tune the counting rules and see how they affect your live eligibility result below.

eligibility.title: Live eligibility
eligibility.eligible: Eligible on your chosen application date
eligibility.notEligible: Not yet eligible on your chosen application date
eligibility.toleranceExceeded: Absences in the trailing 12-month window exceeded the 90-day tolerance.
eligibility.insufficientHistory: Not enough presence history yet to complete a 365-day anniversary window.
eligibility.marriageUnconfirmedNotice: Final-period rules for the marriage route are unconfirmed by research. Only the flat 3-years-married + 2-years-residence figures above are calculated. Verify all other aspects of this route with a Cyprus immigration lawyer before applying.
eligibility.nearestEligibleFound: You could be eligible as early as {{date}}.
eligibility.nearestEligibleNotFound: No eligible date was found within the next 5 years with your current data. Please double check your travel log and ARC date.
eligibility.improvementTitle: You might be able to improve your eligibility date
eligibility.improvementArc: Turning on “include ARC date” could move your nearest eligible date about {{days}} day(s) earlier.
eligibility.improvementBcs: Turning on the BCS concession for {{year}} could move your nearest eligible date about {{days}} day(s) earlier.
eligibility.needMoreData: Enter your ARC date, application date, and travel log to see a live eligibility estimate here.

education.title: How the calculation works
education.description: A plain-language walkthrough of the mechanics behind your result, with the exact rules from the underlying legal research.
education.anniversaryBody: Starting from the day before your intended application date, the calculator scans backward day by day, counting every day you were physically present in Cyprus. It keeps a running tally of present days and absent days. The scan stops the moment present days reach 365. If, at that point, the total absent days encountered during the scan are 90 or fewer, a valid “anniversary year” has been found — its length is simply 365 plus however many absence days it had to stretch to absorb. If absences exceed 90 before 365 present days are reached, no anniversary year exists for that date, and the application date must move later until a fresh scan succeeds.
education.anniversaryExample: Suppose you left Cyprus for a single 30-day trip during the year before your application date, and were present every other day. The scan needs 365 present days, and it also absorbs the 30 absence days it encounters along the way — so the resulting anniversary window is 395 days long (365 + 30), not a strict calendar year. Since 30 is well under the 90-day tolerance, this is still a valid anniversary year.
education.cumulativeBody: Separately from the anniversary year, your route requires a total number of cumulative years of physical presence (3, 4, or 7, depending on route and Greek level) accumulated anywhere within a rolling 10-year window that ends the day before your anniversary year begins.
education.cumulativeInterpretationBody: This is implemented as a running total: all present days anywhere inside the 10-year window are added up (with BCS forgiveness applied where relevant) and compared against the required day count. It is not a requirement that your presence fill one fixed, uninterrupted sub-window immediately before the window ends — gaps and re-entries are fine as long as the total adds up. This is the standard reading used by every researched legal source and is flagged here as the resolved interpretation, since the alternative (a fixed, unbroken sub-window) was tested first and contradicted a verified real-world case.
education.togglesBody: These are two independent mechanisms. The BCS concession (route: fast-track only) forgives up to 90 absence days per calendar year, but only for years you mark as held under BCS-type residence status, and only for the cumulative-years tally. The ARC-date-inclusion toggle is unrelated: it simply decides whether the ARC issuing date itself counts as your first day of presence, or whether counting starts the day after. Both toggles can be turned on independently, and the app will tell you if either would move your nearest eligible date earlier.
education.amendmentBody: An earlier belief held that applicants on the standard (non-fast-track) route needed to be physically present in Cyprus for a full 12 months before applying without leaving even for a single day. Confirmed research (KPMG, Deloitte) shows this was superseded by a December 2023 legislative amendment introducing the same 90-day absence tolerance already used on the fast-track routes. This calculator applies the 90-day tolerance uniformly across fast-track and standard routes for the final anniversary year.

sources.label: European Commission — Cyprus citizenship legislation update (2024)

m126.title: M126 — Registering a minor child as a Cypriot citizen
m126.description: For parents who acquired Cypriot citizenship AFTER their child's birth (by naturalization or by marriage-registration) to register their minor child as a Cypriot citizen.
m126.whereFiled: Filed at the District Office / Civil Registry, signed before a Registrar of a Cypriot Court.
documents: Apostilled birth certificate of the minor
m126.ageCutoffWarning: Sources disagree on the age cutoff for “minor” in this context — some cite 18, others 21. This is flagged as unverified. Confirm the correct age limit with a Cyprus immigration lawyer before relying on it.
m126.counselNote: This card is informational only and is not connected to the eligibility calculator above. Always verify current requirements and the age cutoff with counsel before filing.

export.title: Export your record
export.description: Choose which sections to include, review and edit the text, then generate a PDF matching the reference record format.
export.editableNote: Every text block below is editable before export.
export.marriageNoticeExport: Final-period rules for the marriage route are unconfirmed — verify with counsel.

pdf.title:
Detailed Record of Arrivals and Departures
to and from the Republic of Cyprus
pdf.subtitle: Supporting document for the Cypriot citizenship by naturalization application
pdf.eventsHeading: Chronological record of arrivals and departures
pdf.arcObtainedWithBcsReceipt: ARC obtained, first BCS receipt issued
pdf.referenceDatesHeading: Reference dates (for information only, do not affect the calculation)
pdf.referenceDatesNote: These dates concern the applicant's residence-permit administrative history and are provided for the officer's convenience only; the calculation of days is based exclusively on the applicant's physical presence in Cyprus as evidenced by passport stamps.

reasons.cumulative-shortfall: The cumulative-years requirement is not yet met.
reasons.anniversary-tolerance-exceeded: Absences in the trailing 12-month window exceed the 90-day tolerance.
reasons.greek-level-missing: Greek language level has not been selected.
reasons.marriage-date-missing: Marriage date has not been entered.
reasons.marriage-years-not-met: The 3-years-married requirement has not been met.
reasons.marriage-residence-not-met: The 2-years cumulative residence requirement has not been met.
reasons.marriage-final-period-unconfirmed: Final-period rules for marriage route are unconfirmed — verify with counsel.
